response:
I'll perform clustering on the gene expression data of slice 0 to identify groups of cells with similar expression profiles, then visualize these clusters spatially to highlight potential distinct tissue regions.

```python
import scanpy as sc
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Get the slice
slice_0 = session.get_slice(0)

# Confirm data level
if not slice_0.is_cell_level:
    raise ValueError("This analysis requires cell-level data")

# Preprocess the data
adata = slice_0.adata.copy()  # Work on a copy to avoid modifying original
sc.pp.normalize_total(adata, target_sum=1e4)
sc.pp.log1p(adata)
sc.pp.highly_variable_genes(adata, n_top_genes=2000, flavor='seurat')
adata = adata[:, adata.var.highly_variable]
sc.pp.pca(adata, n_comps=30)
sc.pp.neighbors(adata, n_neighbors=10, n_pcs=30)

# Perform clustering
sc.tl.leiden(adata, resolution=0.5)

# Visualize clusters spatially
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))
scatter = plt.scatter(adata.obs['x'], adata.obs['y'], c=adata.obs['leiden'].astype(int), cmap='tab20', s=1, alpha=0.8)
plt.colorbar(scatter, label='Cluster ID')
plt.title('Spatial Clustering of Cells on Slice 0')
plt.xlabel('x coordinate')
plt.ylabel('y coordinate')
plt.axis('equal')
plt.show()
```

*(1 plot(s) generated)*
